# Source Card: Clinical Practice Guideline for Screening and Management of High Blood Pressure in Children and Adolescents

## Summary
The AAP 2017 clinical practice guideline (Flynn et al., Pediatrics 140(3):e20171904; PMID 28827377) is the primary US standard defining pediatric blood-pressure categories, diagnostic thresholds, and target-organ-damage criteria. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies the canonical, age-banded BP classification (percentile-based for ages 1 to <13 y, static adult-style mmHg cut points for adolescents >=13 y), the revised left-ventricular-hypertrophy definition, and the normative-table derivation basis (normal-weight children only). It supersedes the 2004 Fourth Report and renames "prehypertension" to "elevated blood pressure."

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Terminology: "prehypertension" -> "elevated blood pressure" — locator: Abstract / Sec 3.1 p.6 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d2adc9a3d7a73488cbcb3faa4a8824bedaae4f3092401d7a651882374db40224]"
- (ev_002) Children 1-<13 y categories (percentile + whichever-is-lower mmHg) — locator: Table 3, col 1-13 y, p.6 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cb2fc5cd267e4ea0428d64e66a461eb1cefe723ba65b466761f1ae22920407ee]"
- (ev_003) Adolescents >=13 y static cut points — locator: Table 3, col >=13 y, p.6 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:260c8a952894f795c9591f9eb73e6d824d63ba4b1c7843e230be5780db61b2c8]"
- (ev_004) Diagnosis: >=95th pct auscultatory-confirmed at 3 visits — locator: KAS 3, p.2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:50f281fc475f7b7e13f20b99f7144278e31ced13e0cab7329c95356abd6563be]"
- (ev_005) LVH definition — locator: KAS 15-2, p.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f4f0b11d66965f48ca82c6235838b7c18ce2205935d204730cb1a2c499efd2e1]"
- (ev_006) Normative tables from normal-weight children — locator: Sec 3.2, p.6-7 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ed273032cc911047f5304d15145f1cc5cfe6691581b38194d7383d03cc172db7]"
- (ev_007) Screening cadence/age — locator: KAS 1/2, p.2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a8c516fb93a3c2af101266ace331f37cbd9bd23e849498f4017068af25548d38]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Age bands are load-bearing: percentile-based scheme applies to 1 to <13 y (requires age/sex/height normative tables); static adult-style mmHg scheme applies to >=13 y.
- Assay/method dependence: normative percentiles were collected by an auscultatory technique and may differ from oscillometric or ABPM values; diagnosis requires auscultatory-confirmed readings. LVH is assessed by echocardiography (ECG explicitly not recommended for LVH detection, KAS 14).
- Lifecycle: effective 2017-08; version = AAP 2017 CPG; supersedes the 2004 Fourth Report. The >=13 y scheme is designed to interface with the 2017 ACC/AHA adult HTN guideline.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Stage 2 HTN and secondary/target-organ findings are escalation triggers within the guideline (e.g., echocardiography before pharmacologic treatment; repeat imaging for stage 2 / secondary / incompletely-treated chronic stage 1). This card records the classification/definition thresholds only; it does NOT encode diagnosis, treatment, or dosing directives — those remain out of scope for the CDS proposal.

## Limitations & conflicts
- The adolescent (>=13 y) cut points are a statistical alignment to adult 2017 ACC/AHA thresholds, not derived from pediatric CV-outcome data ("no data to identify a specific level of BP in childhood that leads to adverse CV outcomes"); preserve as a modeling assumption, not an outcome-validated boundary.
- Conflicts preserved, not collapsed: (a) vs the 2004 Fourth Report — different staging terms/tables (included overweight/obese children); (b) vs European (ESH) pediatric definitions that retain percentile-only schemes for adolescents rather than static adult cut points. Do not average across these — a CDS rule must pick one authority and label it.
- Method conflict preserved: auscultatory-derived normatives vs oscillometric/ABPM device readings may disagree; the guideline flags this explicitly.
- GAP — exact Table 4/5 per-age/height/sex normative mmHg cell values were not transcribed here (large multi-page tables); only the derivation basis (quantile regression, BMI <85th pct) and the category boundary rules are asserted. Per-cell percentile values must be sourced directly from Tables 4-7 of the guideline before any numeric CDS rule uses them.
- GAP — the g/m2.7 LVH unit is an allometric height-index (grams per meter^2.7); it is not expressible in standard UCUM and is recorded verbatim as "g/m2.7". A CDS converter must treat height^2.7 indexing explicitly rather than coercing to a standard UCUM unit.
